top_signals.title: Gold Jumps After US and Iran Agree Deal That Would Reopen Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Gold and silver rallied after the US and Iran announced an interim deal to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing global inflation fears and potentially tempering expectations for interest-rate hikes.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Iran War’s ‘Inflationary Legacy’ Key to Stock Rally’s Longevity
top_signals.summary: Inflation worries kept investors cautious even as bonds and stocks rallied on Monday after a US-Iran interim deal eased nerves, with market participants warning that the conflict’s economic fallout remains unresolved.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
